text: Alice and Harold got married on Sep 3rd, 1992. Harold and Alice have a son, born Jan 25th, 2000. Harold died on Feb 28th, 2016. They had been living in the same house since 1993, maintained by Alice. Alice and her son continued doing so after Harold's death. Alice's gross income for the year 2017 was $236422. Alice has employed Bob, Cameron, Dan, Emily, Fred and George for agricultural labor from Sep 9th to Oct 1st 2017, and paid them $5012 each. Alice takes the standard deduction in 2017.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
% Facts from the case
spouse("Alice","Harold").
died("Harold",2016).
not_remarried("Alice",2017).
joint_return_possible("Alice","Harold",2016).

child("Alice","Son").
birth_year("Son",2000).
younger_than("Son","Alice").
principal_abode("Son","Alice",2017).
not_joint_return("Son",2017).

maintains_home("Alice",2017).
half_cost("Alice",2017).

gross_income("Alice",2017,236422).
takes_standard_deduction("Alice",2017).

wage("Alice","Bob",2017,5012,agricultural,cash).
wage("Alice","Cameron",2017,5012,agricultural,cash).
wage("Alice","Dan",2017,5012,agricultural,cash).
wage("Alice","Emily",2017,5012,agricultural,cash).
wage("Alice","Fred",2017,5012,agricultural,cash).
wage("Alice","George",2017,5012,agricultural,cash).

% Utility
sum_list([],0).
sum_list([H|T],S) :- sum_list(T,S1), S is H+S1.

round2(X,R) :- R is round(X*100)/100.

% Age
age(Person,Year,Age) :- birth_year(Person,BY), Age is Year-BY.

% Child relationship
child_of(Child,Parent) :- child(Parent,Child).

% Qualifying child
qualifying_child(T,Child,Year) :-
    child_of(Child,T),
    principal_abode(Child,T,Year),
    age(Child,Year,Age),
    Age < 25,
    younger_than(Child,T),
    not_joint_return(Child,Year).

dependent(T,Child,Year) :- qualifying_child(T,Child,Year).

% Surviving spouse
surviving_spouse(P,Year) :-
    spouse(P,Sp),
    died(Sp,DY),
    (DY is Year-1 ; DY is Year-2),
    not_remarried(P,Year),
    maintains_home(P,Year),
    half_cost(P,Year),
    dependent(P,Child,Year),
    principal_abode(Child,P,Year),
    joint_return_possible(P,Sp,DY).

% Married status
married(P,Year) :-
    spouse(P,Sp),
    ( \+ died(Sp,_) ; (died(Sp,DY), DY >= Year) ).

% Head of household (not used but included)
head_of_household(P,Year) :-
    \+ married(P,Year),
    \+ surviving_spouse(P,Year),
    maintains_home(P,Year),
    half_cost(P,Year),
    dependent(P,Child,Year),
    principal_abode(Child,P,Year).

% Filing status
filing_status(P,Year,surviving_spouse) :- surviving_spouse(P,Year).
filing_status(P,Year,head_of_household) :- head_of_household(P,Year).
filing_status(P,Year,single) :- \+ married(P,Year), \+ surviving_spouse(P,Year), \+ head_of_household(P,Year).

% Standard deduction
base_single(Year,B) :- (Year>=2018,Year<2026 -> B=12000 ; B=3000).
base_hoh(Year,B) :- (Year>=2018,Year<2026 -> B=18000 ; B=4400).

basic_standard_deduction(Status,Year,Amount) :-
    (Status=surviving_spouse -> base_single(Year,B), Amount is 2*B ;
     Status=head_of_household -> base_hoh(Year,Amount) ;
     base_single(Year,Amount)).

additional_standard_deduction(_,_,0).

standard_deduction(P,Year,Amount) :-
    takes_standard_deduction(P,Year),
    filing_status(P,Year,Status),
    basic_standard_deduction(Status,Year,B),
    additional_standard_deduction(P,Year,A),
    Amount is B + A.

% Personal exemptions
exemption_amount(Year,Amount) :- (Year>=2018,Year<2026 -> Amount=0 ; Amount=2000).

num_exemptions(P,Year,Num) :-
    findall(D, dependent(P,D,Year), L),
    length(L,N),
    Num is N + 1.

personal_exemptions(P,Year,Total) :-
    exemption_amount(Year,Ex),
    num_exemptions(P,Year,Num),
    Total is Ex * Num.

% Adjusted gross income
adjusted_gross_income(P,Year,AGI) :- gross_income(P,Year,AGI).

% Taxable income
taxable_income(P,Year,Taxable) :-
    adjusted_gross_income(P,Year,AGI),
    standard_deduction(P,Year,SD),
    personal_exemptions(P,Year,PE),
    Taxable is AGI - SD - PE.

% Income tax brackets
tax_from_brackets(surviving_spouse,TI,Tax) :-
    (TI =< 36900 -> Tax is 0.15*TI ;
     TI =< 89150 -> Tax is 5535 + 0.28*(TI-36900) ;
     TI =< 140000 -> Tax is 20165 + 0.31*(TI-89150) ;
     TI =< 250000 -> Tax is 35928.50 + 0.36*(TI-140000) ;
     Tax is 75528.50 + 0.396*(TI-250000)).
tax_from_brackets(head_of_household,TI,Tax) :-
    (TI =< 29600 -> Tax is 0.15*TI ;
     TI =< 76400 -> Tax is 4440 + 0.28*(TI-29600) ;
     TI =< 127500 -> Tax is 17544 + 0.31*(TI-76400) ;
     TI =< 250000 -> Tax is 33385 + 0.36*(TI-127500) ;
     Tax is 77485 + 0.396*(TI-250000)).
tax_from_brackets(single,TI,Tax) :-
    (TI =< 22100 -> Tax is 0.15*TI ;
     TI =< 53500 -> Tax is 3315 + 0.28*(TI-22100) ;
     TI =< 115000 -> Tax is 12107 + 0.31*(TI-53500) ;
     TI =< 250000 -> Tax is 31172 + 0.36*(TI-115000) ;
     Tax is 79772 + 0.396*(TI-250000)).

income_tax(P,Year,Tax) :-
    filing_status(P,Year,Status),
    taxable_income(P,Year,TI),
    tax_from_brackets(Status,TI,Tax).

% Employer tax for agricultural labor
total_cash_wages_agri(P,Year,Total) :-
    findall(A, wage(P,_,Year,A,agricultural,cash), L),
    sum_list(L,Total).

employer_agricultural(P,Year) :-
    total_cash_wages_agri(P,Year,Total),
    Total >= 20000.

employment_agricultural(P,Year) :- employer_agricultural(P,Year).

capped_wage(A,W) :- (A =< 7000 -> W=A ; W=7000).

wages_for_employment(P,Year,Total) :-
    employment_agricultural(P,Year),
    findall(W, (wage(P,_,Year,A,agricultural,cash), capped_wage(A,W)), L),
    sum_list(L,Total).

employer_tax(P,Year,Tax) :-
    wages_for_employment(P,Year,Total),
    Tax is 0.06*Total.

% Total tax
tax(P,Year,Tax) :-
    income_tax(P,Year,IT),
    (employer_tax(P,Year,ET) -> true ; ET=0),
    Sum is IT + ET,
    round2(Sum,Tax).

:- halt.

Alice and Bob file separately in 2017. Alice takes the standard deduction. Alice and Bob have the same principal place of abode in 2017.

reference_prolog:
% Facts

person(bob).

spouse(alice,bob).
spouse(bob,alice).

birth_year(alice,1950).
birth_year(bob,1951).

income(alice,2017,36266).

filing_status(bob,2017,married_separate).

itemizes(alice,2017,false).
itemizes(bob,2017,false).

joint_return(alice,2017,false).
joint_return(bob,2017,false).

dependent_of(bob,2017,false).

% Helper predicates
age(Person,Year,Age) :-
    birth_year(Person,BY),
    Age is Year - BY.

age_65_or_more(Person,Year) :-
    age(Person,Year,Age),
    Age >= 65.

spouse_exemption_allowed(Taxpayer,Year) :-

    income(Sp,Year,0),

number_exemptions(Taxpayer,Year,Num) :-

exemption_amount(Year,Amount) :-

personal_exemptions(Taxpayer,Year,Total) :-

basic_standard_deduction(Person,Year,Amount) :-

additional_standard_deduction(Person,Year,Amount) :-
    filing_status(Person,Year,married_separate),
    (age_65_or_more(Person,Year) -> A1 is 600 ; A1 is 0),
    (spouse_exemption_allowed(Person,Year),
     spouse(Person,Sp),
     age_65_or_more(Sp,Year) -> A2 is 600 ; A2 is 0),
    Amount is A1 + A2.

standard_deduction(Person,Year,Amount) :-
    filing_status(Person,Year,married_separate),
    spouse(Person,Sp),
    itemizes(Sp,Year,false),
    basic_standard_deduction(Person,Year,Basic),
    additional_standard_deduction(Person,Year,Add),
    Amount is Basic + Add.

adjusted_gross_income(Person,Year,AGI) :-
    income(Person,Year,AGI).

taxable_income(Person,Year,TI) :-
    adjusted_gross_income(Person,Year,AGI),
    standard_deduction(Person,Year,Std),
    personal_exemptions(Person,Year,Ex),

% Tax computation for married filing separate
tax_mfs(TI,Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 18450 ->
        Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 44575 ->
        Tax is 2767.50 + 0.28 * (TI - 18450)
    ; TI =< 70000 ->
        Tax is 10082.50 + 0.31 * (TI - 44575)
    ; TI =< 125000 ->
        Tax is 17964.25 + 0.36 * (TI - 70000)
    ; 
        Tax is 37764.25 + 0.396 * (TI - 125000)
    ).

tax(Person,Year,Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Person,Year,TI),
    tax_mfs(TI,Tax).

text: Charlie is Alice's father since April 15th, 1994. Bob is Charlie's brother since October 12th, 1992. Alice's gross income in 2015 is $87319. Both Charlie and Bob have no income in 2015, and are not qualifying children to any taxpayer.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2015?
reference_prolog:
% ----------------------------
% Statutory reasoning (subset)
% ----------------------------

% ---- Basic financial facts interface ----
gross_income(Taxpayer, Year, Amount) :- fact_gross_income(Taxpayer, Year, Amount).
adjusted_gross_income(Taxpayer, Year, AGI) :- gross_income(Taxpayer, Year, AGI).

% ---- §152: Dependent = qualifying child or qualifying relative ----
dependent(Taxpayer, Dep, Year) :-
    qualifying_relative(Taxpayer, Dep, Year).
dependent(Taxpayer, Dep, Year) :-
    qualifying_child(Taxpayer, Dep, Year).

% (No qualifying child facts in this case; rule included for completeness.)
qualifying_child(_Taxpayer, _Child, _Year) :- fail.

% §152(d)(1): qualifying relative requirements used here:
% (A) relationship per §152(d)(2)
% (B) no income for calendar year
% (D) not a qualifying child of any taxpayer
qualifying_relative(Taxpayer, Dep, Year) :-
    related_for_qr(Dep, Taxpayer),
    income(Dep, Year, Inc), Inc =:= 0,
    not_qualifying_child_of_anyone(Dep, Year).

income(Person, Year, Amount) :- fact_income(Person, Year, Amount).

not_qualifying_child_of_anyone(Dep, Year) :-
    fact_not_qc_any_taxpayer(Dep, Year).

% §152(d)(2) Relationship (only what we need for this problem):
related_for_qr(Dep, Taxpayer) :-
    father(Dep, Taxpayer).
related_for_qr(Dep, Taxpayer) :-
    brother_of_parent(Dep, Taxpayer).

father(Father, Child) :- fact_father(Father, Child).

% §152(d)(2)(F): brother or sister of the father or mother of the taxpayer
brother_of_parent(Uncle, Taxpayer) :-
    father(Parent, Taxpayer),
    brother(Uncle, Parent).

brother(Bro, Sib) :- fact_brother(Bro, Sib).

% ---- §151: personal exemptions (2015 uses $2,000 exemption amount) ----
exemption_amount(Year, 2000) :- Year < 2018.

% Taxpayer exemption (always 1 for an individual taxpayer)
taxpayer_exemptions_count(_Taxpayer, 1).

dependent_exemptions_count(Taxpayer, Year, Count) :-
    findall(Dep, dependent(Taxpayer, Dep, Year), Deps),
    sort(Deps, UniqueDeps),
    length(UniqueDeps, Count).

total_personal_exemptions_amount(Taxpayer, Year, TotalAmt) :-
    exemption_amount(Year, ExAmt),
    taxpayer_exemptions_count(Taxpayer, TaxpayerCount),
    dependent_exemptions_count(Taxpayer, Year, DepCount),
    TotalCount is TaxpayerCount + DepCount,
    TotalAmt is TotalCount * ExAmt.

% ---- §63: standard deduction (2015 amounts: HOH 4400, other 3000, joint/surviving 6000) ----
filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, Status) :- fact_filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, Status).

standard_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, SD) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, joint), !,
    SD is 6000.
standard_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, SD) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, surviving_spouse), !,
    SD is 6000.
standard_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, SD) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, head_of_household), !,
    SD is 4400.
standard_deduction(_Taxpayer, _Year, 3000).

taxable_income(Taxpayer, Year, TI) :-
    adjusted_gross_income(Taxpayer, Year, AGI),
    standard_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, SD),
    total_personal_exemptions_amount(Taxpayer, Year, ExTotal),
    TI0 is AGI - SD - ExTotal,
    (TI0 =< 0 -> TI = 0 ; TI = TI0).

% ---- §1: tax rate schedules (only the ones potentially needed here) ----
tax(Taxpayer, Year, Tax) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, Status),
    taxable_income(Taxpayer, Year, TI),
    tax_by_status(Status, TI, Tax).

% §1(c) Unmarried individuals (not surviving spouse, not head of household)
tax_by_status(unmarried, TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 22100 ->
        Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 53500 ->
        Tax is 3315 + 0.28 * (TI - 22100)
    ; TI =< 115000 ->
        Tax is 12107 + 0.31 * (TI - 53500)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 31172 + 0.36 * (TI - 115000)
    ;   Tax is 79772 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

% (Other statuses omitted; not needed for this case)
tax_by_status(head_of_household, _TI, _Tax) :- fail.
tax_by_status(joint, _TI, _Tax) :- fail.
tax_by_status(surviving_spouse, _TI, _Tax) :- fail.
tax_by_status(married_separate, _TI, _Tax) :- fail.

% ----------------------------
% Case facts
% ----------------------------

% Relationships
fact_father("Charlie", "Alice").
fact_brother("Bob", "Charlie").

% Income facts (2015)
fact_gross_income("Alice", 2015, 87319).
fact_income("Charlie", 2015, 0).
fact_income("Bob", 2015, 0).

% Not qualifying children to any taxpayer (given)
fact_not_qc_any_taxpayer("Charlie", 2015).
fact_not_qc_any_taxpayer("Bob", 2015).

% Filing status (not specified in facts; assume "unmarried" under §1(c))
fact_filing_status("Alice", 2015, unmarried).

% ----------------------------
% Query
% ----------------------------

text: Alice and Bob got married on Feb 3rd, 1992. Alice and Bob have a child, Charlie, born October 9th, 2000. Alice died on July 9th, 2014. From 2004 to 2019, Bob furnished 40% of the costs of maintaining the home where he and Charlie lived during that time. In 2013, Alice and Bob filed jointly, and took the standard deduction. In 2013, Alice earned $65400 and Bob earned $56400.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2013?
reference_prolog:
% Facts from the case
person(alice).
person(bob).
person(charlie).

spouse(alice,bob).
spouse(bob,alice).

joint_return(alice,bob,2013).

income(alice,2013,65400).
income(bob,2013,56400).

birth_year(charlie,2000).
parent(alice,charlie).
parent(bob,charlie).

lived_with(alice,charlie,2013).
lived_with(bob,charlie,2013).

younger(charlie,alice).
younger(charlie,bob).

% Rules for qualifying child (Section 152)
relationship(T,C) :- parent(T,C).

same_abode(T,C,Y) :- lived_with(T,C,Y).

age(C,Y,Age) :- birth_year(C,BY), Age is Y - BY.

age_requirement(T,C,Y) :- age(C,Y,Age), Age < 25, younger(C,T).

qualifying_child(T,C,Y) :-
    relationship(T,C),
    same_abode(T,C,Y),
    age_requirement(T,C,Y),
    \+ joint_return(C,_,Y).

dependent(T,C,Y) :- qualifying_child(T,C,Y).

% Exemption amount (Section 151)
exemption_amount(Year,Amount) :-
    (Year >= 2018, Year < 2026 -> Amount = 0 ; Amount = 2000).

% Count dependents for joint return
count_dependents_joint(P1,P2,Y,Count) :-
    findall(D,(dependent(P1,D,Y); dependent(P2,D,Y)),List),
    sort(List,Unique),
    length(Unique,Count).

exemptions_joint(P1,P2,Y,ExAmount) :-
    exemption_amount(Y,ExAmt),
    count_dependents_joint(P1,P2,Y,Count),
    ExAmount is ExAmt * (2 + Count).

% Standard deduction (Section 63)
basic_standard_deduction(joint,Year,Amount) :-
    (Year >= 2018, Year < 2026 -> Amount is 2*12000 ; Amount is 2*3000).

additional_standard_deduction(_,_,0).

standard_deduction(joint,Year,SD) :-
    basic_standard_deduction(joint,Year,BSD),
    additional_standard_deduction(joint,Year,ASD),
    SD is BSD + ASD.

% AGI and taxable income
agi_joint(P1,P2,Y,AGI) :-
    income(P1,Y,I1),
    income(P2,Y,I2),
    AGI is I1 + I2.

taxable_income_joint(P1,P2,Y,TI) :-
    agi_joint(P1,P2,Y,AGI),
    standard_deduction(joint,Y,SD),
    exemptions_joint(P1,P2,Y,EX),
    Temp is AGI - SD - EX,
    (Temp < 0 -> TI = 0 ; TI = Temp).

% Tax rate for joint return (Section 1(a))
tax_rate_joint(TI,Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 36900 -> Tax is 0.15*TI
    ; TI =< 89150 -> Tax is 5535 + 0.28*(TI-36900)
    ; TI =< 140000 -> Tax is 20165 + 0.31*(TI-89150)
    ; TI =< 250000 -> Tax is 35928.5 + 0.36*(TI-140000)
    ; Tax is 75528.5 + 0.396*(TI-250000)
    ).

% Main tax computation
tax(Person,Y,Tax) :-
    joint_return(Person,Spouse,Y),
    taxable_income_joint(Person,Spouse,Y,TI),
    tax_rate_joint(TI,Tax).

tax(Person,Y,Tax) :-
    joint_return(Spouse,Person,Y),
    taxable_income_joint(Spouse,Person,Y,TI),
    tax_rate_joint(TI,Tax).

text: Alice has been married since April 4th, 2015. Alice files a joint return with her spouse for 2016. Alice's and her spouse's gross income for the year 2016 is $164612. Alice has paid $4525 to Bob for work done in the year 2016. In 2016, Alice has also paid $9832 into a retirement fund for Bob, and $5322 into health insurance for Charlie, who is Alice's father and has retired in 2016. Charlie had no income in 2016. Alice and her spouse take the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2016?
reference_prolog:
% =========================
% Statutory rules (relevant)
% =========================

% ---- Filing status: joint return (for §1(a) rate table) ----
filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, joint) :-
    joint_return(Taxpayer, Year).

% ---- §63 standard deduction (pre-2018 amounts) ----
% §63(c)(2)(A): joint return basic standard deduction = 200% of $3,000 = $6,000
standard_deduction_amount_dollars(Taxpayer, Year, 6000) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, joint),
    Year < 2018.

% (Not using aged/blind additional standard deduction in this case.)

% ---- §152 qualifying relative (only the parts needed here) ----
dependent(Taxpayer, Dep, Year) :-
    qualifying_relative(Taxpayer, Dep, Year).

qualifying_relative(Taxpayer, Dep, Year) :-
    father_of(Dep, Taxpayer),
    no_income(Dep, Year),
    \+ qualifying_child_of_anyone(Dep, Year).

% (Case gives no facts making Charlie a qualifying child of anyone.)
qualifying_child_of_anyone(_Dep, _Year) :- fail.

% ---- §151 personal exemptions (modeled to match typical joint-return treatment) ----
exemption_amount_dollars(Year, 2000) :- Year < 2018.

% For this problem: joint return -> 2 taxpayer/spouse exemptions, plus dependents.
personal_exemptions_amount_dollars(Taxpayer, Year, Amount) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, joint),
    exemption_amount_dollars(Year, Ex),
    findall(D, dependent(Taxpayer, D, Year), Deps),
    length(Deps, NDeps),
    Amount is (2 + NDeps) * Ex.

% ---- §63(b) taxable income when standard deduction taken ----
taxable_income_dollars(Taxpayer, Year, Taxable) :-
    adjusted_gross_income_dollars(Taxpayer, Year, AGI),
    standard_deduction_amount_dollars(Taxpayer, Year, SD),
    personal_exemptions_amount_dollars(Taxpayer, Year, PE),
    Taxable0 is AGI - SD - PE,
    (Taxable0 >= 0 -> Taxable = Taxable0 ; Taxable = 0).

% ---- §1(a) income tax for joint returns / surviving spouse (2016 bracket amounts as given) ----
% Computation done in cents to avoid floating point issues.

income_tax_cents(Taxpayer, Year, TaxCents) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, joint),
    taxable_income_dollars(Taxpayer, Year, TI),
    income_tax_joint_from_table_cents(TI, TaxCents).

income_tax_joint_from_table_cents(TI, TaxCents) :-
    ( TI =< 36900 ->
        % 15%
        TaxCents is TI * 15 * 100 // 100
    ; TI =< 89150 ->
        % $5,535 + 28% of excess over $36,900
        Excess is TI - 36900,
        TaxCents is 553500 + (Excess * 28 * 100 // 100)
    ; TI =< 140000 ->
        % $20,165 + 31% of excess over $89,150
        Excess is TI - 89150,
        TaxCents is 2016500 + (Excess * 31 * 100 // 100)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        % $35,928.50 + 36% of excess over $140,000
        Excess is TI - 140000,
        TaxCents is 3592850 + (Excess * 36 * 100 // 100)
    ;  % over 250,000
        Excess is TI - 250000,
        % $75,528.50 + 39.6% of excess over $250,000
        % 39.6% = 396/1000
        TaxCents is 7552850 + (Excess * 396 * 100 // 1000)
    ).

% ---- FUTA / unemployment excise tax: §3301 imposes 6% of §3306(b) wages paid w.r.t. §3306(c) employment ----
futa_tax_cents(Employer, Year, TaxCents) :-
    employer_for_year(Employer, Year),
    total_futa_wages_dollars(Employer, Year, Wages),
    % §3301 rate = 6%
    TaxCents is Wages * 6 * 100 // 100.

% Employer status for domestic service: §3306(a)(3) cash wages >= $1,000
employer_for_year(Employer, Year) :-
    cash_wages_paid_for_domestic_service_dollars(Employer, _Employee, Year, Cash),
    Cash >= 1000.

% FUTA wages include remuneration for employment, but cap at $7,000 per employee per year (§3306(b)(1)).
% Here, we only need the employee Bob, and only cash wages count because noncash for service not in course
% of employer's trade or business is excluded (§3306(b)(7)).
total_futa_wages_dollars(Employer, Year, Total) :-
    findall(W, futa_wages_for_employee_capped_dollars(Employer, Year, W), Ws),
    sum_list(Ws, Total).

futa_wages_for_employee_capped_dollars(Employer, Year, Capped) :-
    employee_of(Employer, Emp, Year),
    futa_wages_employee_dollars(Employer, Emp, Year, Wages),
    (Wages =< 7000 -> Capped = Wages ; Capped = 7000).

% Only cash domestic-service wages provided in the case (noncash retirement contribution excluded by §3306(b)(7))
futa_wages_employee_dollars(Employer, Emp, Year, Wages) :-

% Define rules for marital status
married(_, _) :- fail.
surviving_spouse(_, _) :- fail.

% Define adjusted gross income (no adjustments)
adjusted_gross_income(Person, Year, AGI) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, AGI).

% Define filing status: unmarried if not married, not head of household, not surviving spouse
filing_status(Person, Year, unmarried) :-
    not_married(Person, Year),
    \+ head_of_household(Person, Year),
    \+ surviving_spouse(Person, Year).

% Define head of household (not satisfied in this case)
head_of_household(Person, Year) :-
    not_married(Person, Year),
    \+ surviving_spouse(Person, Year),
    maintains_household(Person, Year),
    dependent(Person, Dependent, Year),
    lives_with(Person, Dependent, Year),
    \+ married(Dependent, Year).

% Since the case does not state that Alice pays over half the cost,
% we define maintains_household to fail.
maintains_household(_, _) :- fail.

% Define standard deduction amounts for 2017
standard_deduction(2017, unmarried, 3000).
standard_deduction(2017, head_of_household, 4400).

% Define base exemption amount for 2017
base_exemption_amount(2017, 2000).

% Define applicable amount for phaseout (§68(b))
applicable_amount(2017, unmarried, 250000).
applicable_amount(2017, head_of_household, 275000).

% Define exemption count: taxpayer + dependents
exemption_count(Person, Year, Count) :-
    ((not_married(Person, Year) ; married(Person, Year)) -> Count1 = 1 ; Count1 = 0),
    findall(Dep, dependent(Person, Dep, Year), Deps),
    length(Deps, DepCount),
    Count is Count1 + DepCount.

% Define exemption amount (including phaseout)
exemption_amount(Person, Year, ExemptionAmount) :-
    base_exemption_amount(Year, Base),
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    applicable_amount(Year, Status, ApplicableAmount),
    exemption_count(Person, Year, Count),
    adjusted_gross_income(Person, Year, AGI),
    (   AGI =< ApplicableAmount
    ->  ExemptionAmount is Base * Count
    ;   Excess is AGI - ApplicableAmount,
        Increments is ceil(Excess / 2500),
        ApplicablePercentage is min(100, 2 * Increments),
        TotalBase is Base * Count,
        ExemptionAmount is TotalBase * (1 - ApplicablePercentage / 100)
    ).

% Define taxable income
taxable_income(Person, Year, TaxableIncome) :-
    adjusted_gross_income(Person, Year, AGI),
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    standard_deduction(Year, Status, StdDed),
    exemption_amount(Person, Year, ExemptionAmount),
    TaxableIncome is AGI - StdDed - ExemptionAmount.

% Define tax brackets for unmarried individuals (§1(c))
tax_bracket(unmarried, 2017, 0, 22100, 0.15, 0).
tax_bracket(unmarried, 2017, 22100, 53500, 0.28, 3315).
tax_bracket(unmarried, 2017, 53500, 115000, 0.31, 12107).
tax_bracket(unmarried, 2017, 115000, 250000, 0.36, 31172).
tax_bracket(unmarried, 2017, 250000, 999999999, 0.396, 79772).

% Define compute tax
compute_tax(Status, Year, TI, Tax) :-
    tax_bracket(Status, Year, Lower, Upper, Rate, Base),
    TI > Lower,
    TI =< Upper,
    !,
    Tax is Base + Rate * (TI - Lower).

% Define tax predicate
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    compute_tax(Status, Year, TI, Tax).

% Query to compute and print tax for Alice in 2017

:- halt.

married_status(_P, _Y) :- fail.
surviving_spouse_status(_P, _Y) :- fail.

/* §152(d)(1): relationship + zero income + not qualifying child */

relationship_qualifying_relative(A, B) :-

sum_list([H|T], S) :- sum_list(T, S0), S is H + S0.

payment_(_) :- fail.
deduction_(_) :- fail.
marriage_(_) :- fail.
joint_return_(_) :- fail.
death_(_) :- fail.
birth_(_) :- fail.
blindness_(_) :- fail.

brother_(_) :- fail.
sister_(_) :- fail.

amount_(_,_) :- fail.

end_(_,_) :- fail.
purpose_(_,_) :- fail.

gross_income_individual(Person, Year, Gross) :-

          agent_(Inc, Person),
          amount_(Inc, Amt),
          start_(Inc, S),
          within_year(S, Year)

    gross_income_individual(Person, Year, Gross).

% sibling relationship via brother/sister events
is_sibling_of(X, Y) :-
    (brother_(Rel) ; sister_(Rel)),
    agent_(Rel, X),
    patient_(Rel, Y),
    X \== Y.
is_sibling_of(X, Y) :-

    agent_(Rel, Y),
    patient_(Rel, X),
    X \== Y.

% requires birth facts for BOTH dependent and taxpayer to prove "younger than taxpayer".
s152_c(Child, Taxp, Year) :-

    residence_(R1), agent_(R1, Child), patient_(R1, Home),
    residence_(R2), agent_(R2, Taxp),  patient_(R2, Home),
    start_(R1, S1), within_year(S1, Year),
    start_(R2, S2), within_year(S2, Year),

s152_d(Rel, Taxp, Year) :-

    ; s152_d(Dep, Taxp, Year)

exemption_amount_base(_Year, 2000).

exemption_amount_after_phaseout(Taxp, Year, Ea) :-

/* ---------- FACTS FOR THE CASE ---------- */
person(bob).
person(alice).

% marriage fact   married(Person1, Person2, Year).
married(bob, alice, 2017).

% In 2019 Bob and Alice file a single joint return and take the standard deduction.

return_type(alice, 2019, joint_standard).

/* ---------- AUXILIARY RULES ---------- */

% spouse(X,Y) is symmetric
spouse(X, Y) :- married(X, Y, _).
spouse(X, Y) :- married(Y, X, _).

% total_income_for_joint(Person, Year, TotalIncome)
% sums the gross income of the person and the spouse for the given year
total_income_for_joint(P, Year, Total) :-
    spouse(P, S),
    gross_income(P, Year, Inc1),
    gross_income(S, Year, Inc2),
    Total is Inc1 + Inc2.

% standard_deduction(Year, FilingType, Amount)
% We only need the amount for a joint return in 2019 taking the standard deduction.
% §63(c)(2)(A): basic SD is 200% of amount in (C).
% §63(c)(7): for 2018-2025 substitute $12,000 for $3,000 in sub-para (C).

% taxable_income(Person, Year, TaxableIncome)
taxable_income(P, Year, TI) :-
    return_type(P, Year, joint_standard),
    total_income_for_joint(P, Year, GI),

    TI is GI - SD.

% joint_bracket_tax(TaxableIncome, Tax)
% Implements §1(a) brackets (married filing jointly & surviving spouses)
joint_bracket_tax(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 36900 ->
        Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 89150 ->
        Tax is 5535 + 0.28 * (TI - 36900)
    ; TI =< 140000 ->

    ; TI =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 35928.5 + 0.36 * (TI - 140000)
    ;                           % TI  > 250,000
        Tax is 75528.5 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

% top-level predicate
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    joint_bracket_tax(TI, Tax).

text: Alice got married on Dec 10th, 2009. Alice's gross income for the year 2016 is $554313. Alice files separately and takes the standard deduction. Her husband's gross income in 2016 is $56298 and he takes itemized deductions of $4421.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2016?
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- FACTS DESCRIBING THE CASE ---------- */

% identity
person("Alice").

% year concerned
year(2016).

% incomes
gross_income("Alice", 2016, 554313).
gross_income("Alice_spouse", 2016, 56298).

% filing choices
filing_status("Alice", 2016, married_filing_separate).
uses_standard_deduction("Alice", 2016).          % Alice wants to take it
itemizes_deductions("Alice_spouse", 2016).       % spouse itemizes

% personal data
age("Alice", 2016, 30).      % not aged
blind("Alice", 2016, false). % not blind

/* ---------- STATUTORY RULES NEEDED FOR THE COMPUTATION ---------- */

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%  1.  Standard deduction  (I.R.C. §63(c))
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

% basic amount for married-filing-separate (pre-2018 rules)
basic_standard_deduction_amount(married_filing_separate, 3000).

% §63(c)(6)(A) – standard deduction denied if either spouse itemizes
standard_deduction(Person, Year, 0) :-
    filing_status(Person, Year, married_filing_separate),
    itemizes_deductions(_, Year),                 % SOME spouse itemizes
    !.                                            % rule takes precedence

% Otherwise take the normal basic standard deduction (no aged/blind add-ons needed here)
standard_deduction(Person, Year, Amount) :-
    filing_status(Person, Year, married_filing_separate),
    basic_standard_deduction_amount(married_filing_separate, Amount).

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%  2.  Personal exemption and phase-out (I.R.C. §151(d)(3))
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

% base exemption (pre-2018)
base_exemption_amount(2000).

% section 68(b) applicable amount for married-filing-separate
phaseout_threshold(married_filing_separate, 150000).

% compute one exemption after phase-out
one_exemption_after_phaseout(AGI, married_filing_separate, FinalExemption) :-
    base_exemption_amount(Base),
    phaseout_threshold(married_filing_separate, Threshold),
    Excess is max(0, AGI - Threshold),
    Units is ceiling(Excess / 1250),          % $1,250 increments
    Percent is min(100, Units * 2),           % 2 percentage points per unit
    FinalExemption is Base * (100 - Percent) / 100.

% total exemption deduction for the taxpayer (no spouse deduction allowed here)
total_personal_exemption(Person, Year, Total) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, AGI),
    filing_status(Person, Year, married_filing_separate),
    one_exemption_after_phaseout(AGI, married_filing_separate, OneEx),
    % only the taxpayer herself qualifies for an exemption
    Total is OneEx.

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%  3.  Taxable income  (I.R.C. §63)
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

taxable_income(Person, Year, TaxableIncome) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, AGI),
    standard_deduction(Person, Year, StdDed),
    total_personal_exemption(Person, Year, PersEx),
    TaxableIncome is AGI - StdDed - PersEx.

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%  4.  Tax computation – Married Filing Separate brackets
%      (I.R.C. §1(d))
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

mfs_tax(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 18450 ->
        Tax is TI * 0.15
    ; TI =< 44575 ->
        Tax is 2767.5 + (TI - 18450) * 0.28
    ; TI =< 70000 ->
        Tax is 10082.5 + (TI - 44575) * 0.31
    ; TI =< 125000 ->
        Tax is 17964.25 + (TI - 70000) * 0.36
    ; % over 125,000
      Tax is 37764.25 + (TI - 125000) * 0.396
    ).

tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    mfs_tax(TI, Tax).

/* ---------- QUERY REQUIRED BY THE TASK ---------- */

:- tax("Alice", 2016, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

/* ----------  FACTS FROM THE CASE  ---------- */
gross_income("Alice",2014,718791).

/* Alice and Bob were married 2/3/1997 – 10/30/2001            */
/* We record the calendar years in which the marriage existed. */
married_year("Alice","Bob",Y) :- between(1997,2001,Y).

    married_year(Person,_,Year).

filing_status(Person,Year,surviving_spouse) :-  % death must be within 2 yrs – none here
    fail.                                       
filing_status(Person,Year,head_of_household) :- % no dependents given – none here
    fail.
filing_status(Person,Year,unmarried) :-
    \+ married_in_year(Person,Year).

/* ----------  STANDARD DEDUCTION  ---------- */
standard_deduction(unmarried,Year,Amt) :-
    (Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025 -> Amt = 12000          % §63(c)(7)(C)
    ;                               Amt = 3000).        % §63(c)(2)(C)

/* ----------  PERSONAL EXEMPTION  ---------- */
base_exemption_amount(Year,0)  :- Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025.  % §151(d)(5)
base_exemption_amount(Year,2000) :- Year < 2018 ; Year > 2025.

applicable_amount(unmarried,250000).
applicable_amount(head_of_household,275000).
applicable_amount(joint,300000).
applicable_amount(surviving_spouse,300000).

personal_exemption(Status,Year,AGI,ExAmt) :-
    base_exemption_amount(Year,Base),
    (Base =:= 0 -> ExAmt = 0                                   % years 2018-25
    ; applicable_amount(Status,Lim),
      (AGI =< Lim -> ExAmt = Base                              % no phase-out
      ; Excess is AGI - Lim,
        Increments is ceiling(Excess / 2500),
        Percentage is min(100, Increments * 2),                % §151(d)(3)
        ExAmt is Base * (100 - Percentage) / 100
      )).

    gross_income(Person,Year,GI),
    filing_status(Person,Year,Status),
    standard_deduction(Status,Year,SD),
    personal_exemption(Status,Year,GI,PE),

    tax_bracket_unmarried(_,Lower,Upper,Rate,Base),

    ; TI =< Upper, TI > Lower),
    Tax is Base + Rate * (TI - Lower).

/* ----------  MASTER TAX PREDICATE  ---------- */
tax(Person,Year,Tax) :-
    filing_status(Person,Year,unmarried),          % this case only covers unmarried

% Surviving spouse & head-of-household are not relevant here
surviving_spouse(_, _) :- fail.

    unit(Status, U),
    Units is ceiling(Excess / U),
    PhasePct is min(100, Units * 2),
    Deduction is EA * N * (100 - PhasePct) / 100.

exemptions_count(T, Y, 2) :- spouse_no_income(T, Y), !.
exemptions_count(_, _, 1).

income_tax(mfs, TI, Tax) :-

question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?

/*  ----------  FACTS  ----------  */
gross_income("Alice", 2017, 433320).
gross_income("Bob",   2017, 532134).

spouse("Alice", "Bob").
spouse("Bob",   "Alice").

files_joint_return("Alice", 2017).
files_joint_return("Bob",   2017).

/*  ----------  STANDARD DEDUCTION (2017)  ----------  */
standard_deduction(joint_return, 6000).

/*  ----------  PERSONAL EXEMPTION BASE AMOUNT  ----------  */
personal_exemption_base(Year, 0)    :- Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025.
personal_exemption_base(Year, 2000) :- Year < 2018 ; Year > 2025.

/*  ----------  APPLICABLE AMOUNTS FOR PHASE-OUT  ----------  */
applicable_amount(joint_return, 300000).

/*  ----------  NUMBER OF EXEMPTIONS ON A JOINT RETURN  ----------  */
num_exemptions(joint_return, 2).

/*  ----------  AUXILIARY ­— CEILING DIVISION  ----------  */
ceil_div(N, D, R) :- R is (N + D - 1) // D.

/*  ----------  PERSONAL EXEMPTION AFTER PHASE-OUT  ----------  */
total_personal_exemption(joint_return, Year, AGI, Total) :-
    personal_exemption_base(Year, Base),
    num_exemptions(joint_return, N),
    (   Base =:= 0
    ->  Total = 0
    ;   applicable_amount(joint_return, Threshold),
        (   AGI =< Threshold
        ->  Total is N * Base
        ;   Excess is AGI - Threshold,
            ceil_div(Excess, 2500, Steps),
            Percent is Steps * 2,                       % percent
            ( Percent >= 100
            -> ReductionFactor = 1
            ;  ReductionFactor is Percent / 100 ),
            AllowedPer is Base * (1 - ReductionFactor),
            Total is N * AllowedPer
        )
    ).

/*  ----------  JOINT BRACKET TAX (IRC §1(a))  ----------  */
joint_bracket_tax(TI, Tax) :-
    (   TI =< 36900
    ->  Tax is TI * 0.15
    ;   TI =< 89150
    ->  Tax is 5535 + (TI - 36900) * 0.28
    ;   TI =< 140000
    ->  Tax is 20165 + (TI - 89150) * 0.31
    ;   TI =< 250000
    ->  Tax is 35928.5 + (TI - 140000) * 0.36
    ;   Tax is 75528.5 + (TI - 250000) * 0.396
    ).

/*  ----------  MAIN TAX COMPUTATION  ----------  */
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    files_joint_return(Person, Year),
    spouse(Person, Spouse),
    gross_income(Person, Year, GI1),
    gross_income(Spouse, Year, GI2),
    CombinedGI is GI1 + GI2,
    standard_deduction(joint_return, SD),
    total_personal_exemption(joint_return, Year, CombinedGI, Exemptions),
    TaxableIncome is CombinedGI - SD - Exemptions,

question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2013?

/*********************************************************************
  Statutory rules (only the provisions that matter for this problem)
*********************************************************************/
% exemption amount §151(d)(1)
exemption_amount(2000).

% standard deduction §63(c)(2)(A)
% For a married couple filing a joint return
basic_standard_deduction(married_filing_joint, 6000).

% tax-rate schedule §1(a) (married filing jointly & surviving spouses)
mfj_tax(Taxable, Tax) :-
    (   Taxable =< 36900 ->
        Tax is 0.15 * Taxable
    ;   Taxable =< 89150 ->
        Tax is 5535 + 0.28 * (Taxable - 36900)
    ;   Taxable =< 140000 ->
        Tax is 20165 + 0.31 * (Taxable - 89150)
    ;   Taxable =< 250000 ->
        Tax is 35928.5 + 0.36 * (Taxable - 140000)
    ;   % over 250,000
        Tax is 75528.5 + 0.396 * (Taxable - 250000)
    ).

/*********************************************************************
  Family relationships – minimal facts needed for this case
*********************************************************************/
spouse(alice,bob).
spouse(bob,alice).

child_of(charlie,alice).
child_of(charlie,bob).

born(charlie,2000).

/*********************************************************************
  Basic facts for the 2013 taxable year
*********************************************************************/
income(alice,2013,71879).
income(bob  ,2013,11213).

% Filing choice for 2013
filing_status(alice,2013,married_filing_joint).

% Charlie lives with both parents for the whole year, so he is a
% qualifying child (§152(c)) and therefore a dependent (§152(a)(1))
qualifying_child(Child,Taxpayer,Year) :-
    child_of(Child,Taxpayer),
    age(Child,Year,Age),
    Age < 25.                   % §152(c)(3)

age(Person,Year,Age) :-
    born(Person,BirthYear),
    Age is Year - BirthYear.

dependent_of(Dep,Taxpayer,Year) :-
    qualifying_child(Dep,Taxpayer,Year).

/*********************************************************************
  Computing deductions
*********************************************************************/
standard_deduction(Taxpayer,Year,SD) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer,Year,married_filing_joint),
    basic_standard_deduction(married_filing_joint,SD).

personal_exemptions(Taxpayer,Year,Count) :-
    % two spouses on a joint return + number of dependents
    findall(D,dependent_of(D,Taxpayer,Year),Deps),
    length(Deps,DepCnt),
    Count is 2 + DepCnt.

personal_exemption_deduction(Taxpayer,Year,Amount) :-
    personal_exemptions(Taxpayer,Year,Count),
    exemption_amount(EA),
    Amount is Count * EA.

/*********************************************************************
  Taxable income and tax owed for a joint return
*********************************************************************/
adjusted_gross_income(Taxpayer,Year,AGI) :-
    income(Taxpayer,Year,Inc1),
    spouse(Taxpayer,Spouse),
    income(Spouse,Year,Inc2),
    AGI is Inc1 + Inc2.

taxable_income(Taxpayer,Year,TI) :-
    adjusted_gross_income(Taxpayer,Year,AGI),
    standard_deduction(Taxpayer,Year,SD),
    personal_exemption_deduction(Taxpayer,Year,PE),
    TI is AGI - SD - PE.

tax(Taxpayer,Year,Tax) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer,Year,married_filing_joint),
    taxable_income(Taxpayer,Year,TI),
    mfj_tax(TI,Tax).

  Query – 2013 tax owed by Alice
*********************************************************************/

/* ----------  Statutory rules relevant to this problem ---------- */

income_tax_rate_unmarried(TI, Tax) :-

% Alice’s marital status

text: Alice shares a house with her father Bob since 2007. Alice pays for 75% of the costs of the house, while Charlie, her brother, pays for the remaining 25%. Alice's gross income for the year 2012 was $67285 and Charlie's was $56174. Alice is allowed itemized deductions of $17817.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2012?
reference_prolog:
/************************************************************************
 *  Statutory rules (only the portions needed for this case)            *
 ************************************************************************/

% filing status ---------------------------------------------------------
% A taxpayer is a head-of-household if (i) not married, (ii) not a
% surviving spouse, (iii) maintains the household which is the
% principal place of abode of a dependent father or mother for the
% year, and (iv) furnishes over half of the cost of maintaining it.

head_of_household(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    marital_status(Taxpayer, Year, single),        % not married
    \+ surviving_spouse(Taxpayer, Year),           % not surviving spouse
    dependent(Taxpayer, Parent, Year),             % has a dependent
    parent_of(Parent, Taxpayer),                   % who is father/mother
    maintains_household(Taxpayer, Year).           % paid > ½ the cost

% For this problem nobody is a surviving spouse.
surviving_spouse(_, _) :- fail.

% A taxpayer maintains the household if he/she pays > ½ the cost.
maintains_household(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    share_of_cost(Taxpayer, Year, Share),
    Share > 0.5.

% personal-exemption ----------------------------------------------------
exemption_amount(2000).

num_exemptions(Taxpayer, Year, Num) :-
    findall(D, dependent(Taxpayer, D, Year), L),
    length(L, NDeps),
    Num is NDeps + 1.                % +1 for the taxpayer

total_exemption_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, Amount) :-
    exemption_amount(EA),
    num_exemptions(Taxpayer, Year, Num),
    Amount is EA * Num.

% taxable income --------------------------------------------------------
taxable_income(Taxpayer, Year, TaxableIncome) :-
    gross_income(Taxpayer, Year, GI),
    itemized_deductions(Taxpayer, Year, ID),
    total_exemption_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, Exempt),
    TI is GI - ID - Exempt,
    (  TI > 0 -> TaxableIncome = TI
    ;  TaxableIncome = 0).

% §1(b) – tax table for heads of household
tax_amount_hoh(TI, Tax) :-
    (  TI =< 29600
       -> Tax is TI * 0.15
    ;  TI =< 76400
       -> Tax is 4440 + 0.28 * (TI - 29600)
    ;  TI =< 127500
       -> Tax is 17544 + 0.31 * (TI - 76400)
    ;  TI =< 250000
       -> Tax is 33385 + 0.36 * (TI - 127500)
    ;  Tax is 77485 + 0.396 * (TI - 250000)
    ).

% master rule -----------------------------------------------------------
tax(Taxpayer, Year, Tax) :-
    head_of_household(Taxpayer, Year),
    taxable_income(Taxpayer, Year, TI),
    tax_amount_hoh(TI, Tax).


/************************************************************************
 *  Facts for the Alice / Bob / Charlie case                            *
 ************************************************************************/

% family relationships
parent_of(bob, alice).

% marital status
marital_status(alice, 2012, single).

% household cost shares (fractions)
share_of_cost(alice,  2012, 0.75).
share_of_cost(charlie,2012, 0.25).

% dependent information
dependent(alice, bob, 2012).

% incomes and deductions
gross_income(alice, 2012, 67285).
itemized_deductions(alice, 2012, 17817).

/************************************************************************
 *  Query required by the assignment                                    *
 ************************************************************************/

:- tax(alice, 2012, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

text: Bob and Alice got married on Feb 3rd, 1992. Bob and Alice have a child, Charlie, born October 9th, 2000. Bob died on July 9th, 2014. From 2004 to 2019, Alice furnished the costs of maintaining the home where he and Charlie lived during that time. Alice's gross income for the year 2017 is $25561. Alice takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2017?
reference_prolog:
/* =========  Statutory rules (minimal subset needed for 2017 computation) ========= */

/* --- basic facts about exemption and standard-deduction dollar amounts that apply
       for the 2017 taxable year (pre-TCJA amounts) --- */

exemption_amount(2017, 2000).

std_deduction(head_of_household, 2017, 4400).
std_deduction(surviving_spouse,  2017, 6000).   /* not used here but stated for completeness */
std_deduction(unmarried,         2017, 3000).
std_deduction(married_joint,     2017, 6000).
std_deduction(married_separate,  2017, 3000).

/* --- income-tax brackets for 2017 (§1) ------------------------------ */
tax_rate(head_of_household, 2017, 0,       29600, 0.15,   0).   % 15% of taxable income ≤ $29,600

/* =========  General predicates to turn statutory language into code  ========= */

/* years between two years (difference is D) -------------------------------------------------- */
years_between(Year1, Year2, D) :- D is Year1 - Year2.

/* share of household costs test (§2) : here supplied directly as a fact ------------- */
maintains_household(Taxpayer, Year) :- household_costs_supplied_over_half(Taxpayer, Year).

/* qualifying-child test (stripped down to what we need here) ----------------------- */
qualifying_child(Child, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    child_of(Child, Taxpayer),
    born(Child, date(BYear, _, _)),
    Age is Year - BYear,
    Age =< 24,                                    % §152(c)(3) – age test
    same_principal_abode(Child, Taxpayer, Year),  % §152(c)(1)(B)
    \+ child_filed_joint_return(Child, Year).     % §152(c)(1)(E) – assumed false here

/* dependent (§152) – here we only need qualifying-child dependents */
dependent_of(Dep, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    qualifying_child(Dep, Taxpayer, Year).

/* how many personal-exemption units a taxpayer gets (§151) ------------------------ */
personal_exemption_total(Taxpayer, Year, Total) :-
    exemption_amount(Year, Amt),
    findall(Dep, dependent_of(Dep, Taxpayer, Year), Deps),
    length(Deps, NDeps),
    Total is (1 + NDeps) * Amt.    % 1 for the taxpayer + each dependent

/* surviving-spouse test (§2(a)) ---------------------------------------------------- */
surviving_spouse(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    married_to(Taxpayer, Spouse),
    died(Spouse, date(DYear, _, _)),
    years_between(Year, DYear, Diff),
    Diff >= 0, Diff =< 1,                       % death in either of two years preceding
    maintains_household(Taxpayer, Year),
    dependent_of(_, Taxpayer, Year),            % household must be principal place of abode
    \+ remarried(Taxpayer, Year).

/* head-of-household test (§2(b)) --------------------------------------------------- */
head_of_household(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    \+ married_at_close(Taxpayer, Year),        % not married (§2(b)(1))
    \+ surviving_spouse(Taxpayer, Year),
    maintains_household(Taxpayer, Year),
    ( dependent_of(_, Taxpayer, Year)           % qualifying child or other dependent
    ).

/* Determine filing status for 2017 ------------------------------------------------- */
filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, head_of_household) :- head_of_household(Taxpayer, Year), !.
filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, surviving_spouse)  :- surviving_spouse(Taxpayer, Year),  !.
filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, unmarried).            % fall-back for this simplified code

/* taxable-income computation (§63) ------------------------------------------------- */
taxable_income(Taxpayer, Year, Taxable) :-
    gross_income(Taxpayer, Year, GI),
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, FS),
    std_deduction(FS, Year, SD),
    personal_exemption_total(Taxpayer, Year, PE),
    Taxable is GI - SD - PE.

/* compute tax from taxable income using first bracket only (all we need here) ------ */
income_tax(Taxpayer, Year, Tax) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, FS),
    taxable_income(Taxpayer, Year, Taxable0),
    Taxable is max(0, Taxable0),                % cannot be negative
    tax_rate(FS, Year, Lower, Upper, Rate, Base),
    Taxable =< Upper, Taxable >= Lower,
    Tax is Base + Rate * (Taxable - Lower).

/* =========  Case facts for Alice ================================================== */

% family structure ----------------------------------------------------
married_to(alice, bob).
married_to(bob, alice).

child_of(charlie, alice).
child_of(charlie, bob).

born(charlie, date(2000,10,9)).

% Bob's death ---------------------------------------------------------
died(bob, date(2014,7,9)).

% Household & support facts ------------------------------------------
same_principal_abode(charlie, alice, 2017).
household_costs_supplied_over_half(alice, 2017).

% marital status events ----------------------------------------------
remarried(alice, 2017) :- fail.   % Alice did NOT remarry
married_at_close(Taxpayer, Year) :-     % a taxpayer is married at close of year iff
    married_to(Taxpayer, Spouse),
    \+ died(Spouse, date(DYear,_,_)),
    DYear =< Year, Year =< DYear,       % (ensures spouse alive that year – quick and dirty)
    true.                               % (here, Bob had died in 2014, so this fails for Alice)

% dependents’ joint-return & other bars (assume none) -----------------
child_filed_joint_return(_, _) :- fail.

% income --------------------------------------------------------------
gross_income(alice, 2017, 25561).

/* =========  Wrapper predicate requested by the assignment ========= */

tax(Taxpayer, Year, Tax) :- income_tax(Taxpayer, Year, Tax).

/* =========  Produce the answer for Alice, 2017 ==================== */
:- tax(alice, 2017, Tax), format('Tax result: ~w~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

From 2015 to 2019, Bob furnished the costs of maintaining the home where he and his friend Charlie lived during that time, while Charlie had no income and was not the qualifying child of any taxpayer. Bob earned $304598 every year from 2015 to 2019.
question: How much tax does Bob have to pay in 2018?
reference_prolog:
/* -------------  Facts from the case ------------- */
income(bob, 2018, 304598).

spouse_of(bob, alice, date(1992,2,3)).
spouse_death(alice, 2014).

% Bob and Charlie share a home 2015-2019, Bob pays the costs
maintains_household(bob, charlie, Year) :-
    member(Year,[2015,2016,2017,2018,2019]).

% Charlie is Bob's dependent in those same years (§152(d)(2)(H))
dependent_of(bob, charlie, Year) :-
    maintains_household(bob, charlie, Year).

qualifies_by_H(bob, charlie, Year) :-  % qualifies only under §152(d)(2)(H)
    dependent_of(bob, charlie, Year).

/* -------------  Status rules (simplified to what we need) ------------- */
spouse_alive(Person, Year) :-
    spouse_of(Person, Spouse, _),
    ( \+ spouse_death(Spouse, _) ;  (spouse_death(Spouse,DY), DY > Year) ).

married(Person, Year) :- spouse_alive(Person, Year).      % §7703(a)

% No dependent child ⇒ never a surviving spouse for Bob
surviving_spouse(Person, Year) :-
    spouse_of(Person, Spouse, _),
    spouse_death(Spouse, DY),
    (Year-DY =:= 1 ; Year-DY =:= 2),
    fail.   % (no qualifying child)                       §2(a)

% Head-of-household disallowed when the only dependent is via §152(d)(2)(H)
head_of_household(Person, Year) :-
    \+ married(Person, Year),
    \+ surviving_spouse(Person, Year),
    maintains_household(Person, Dep, Year),
    dependent_of(Person, Dep, Year),
    \+ qualifies_by_H(Person, Dep, Year).                 % §2(b)(3)(B)

status(Person, Year, married_joint)       :- married(Person, Year).
status(Person, Year, surviving_spouse)    :- surviving_spouse(Person, Year).
status(Person, Year, head_of_household)   :- head_of_household(Person, Year).
status(Person, Year, single) :-            % §1(c)
    \+ status(Person, Year, married_joint),
    \+ status(Person, Year, surviving_spouse),
    \+ status(Person, Year, head_of_household).

/* -------------  Deductions (2018–2025 rules) ------------- */
standard_deduction_by_status(married_joint,  _, 24000).
standard_deduction_by_status(surviving_spouse,_, 24000).
standard_deduction_by_status(head_of_household,_, 18000).
standard_deduction_by_status(single,         _, 12000).   % §63(c)(7)

standard_deduction(Person, Year, SD) :-
    Year>=2018, Year=<2025,
    status(Person, Year, Status),
    standard_deduction_by_status(Status, Year, SD).

personal_exemption(_, Year, 0) :-        % §151(d)(5)
    Year>=2018, Year=<2025.

/* -------------  Taxable income ------------- */
taxable_income(Person, Year, TI) :-
    income(Person, Year, GI),
    standard_deduction(Person, Year, SD),
    personal_exemption(Person, Year, PE),
    TI is GI - SD - PE.

/* -------------  §1(c) single rate schedule ------------- */
single_tax(TI, Tax) :-
    ( TI =< 22100 ->                 Tax is 0.15 * TI
    ; TI =< 53500 ->                 Tax is 3315   + 0.28 * (TI-22100)
    ; TI =< 115000 ->                Tax is 12107  + 0.31 * (TI-53500)
    ; TI =< 250000 ->                Tax is 31172  + 0.36 * (TI-115000)
    ;                                 Tax is 79772 + 0.396* (TI-250000)
    ).

/* -------------  Overall tax ------------- */
tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    status(Person, Year, single),         % Bob is single in 2018
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    single_tax(TI, Tax).

/* -------------  Query required by the task ------------- */
:- tax(bob, 2018, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

Alice takes the standard deduction.
question: How much tax does Alice have to pay in 2010?
reference_prolog:
/* =========  STATUTORY RULES  ========= */

% ---------- BASIC MONETARY AMOUNTS ----------
exemption_amount(2000).

standard_basic(joint,          6000).   % not used here, shown for completeness
standard_basic(surviving,      6000).
standard_basic(head_of_house,  4400).
standard_basic(single,         3000).
standard_basic(separate,       3000).

% ---------- INCOME-TAX BRACKETS FOR 2010 ----------
% tax_bracket(Status, LowerLimit, UpperLimitOrInf, BaseTax, MarginalRate).
tax_bracket(single, 0,       22100,     0,        0.15).
tax_bracket(single, 22100,   53500,     3315,     0.28).
tax_bracket(single, 53500,   115000,    12107,    0.31).
tax_bracket(single, 115000,  250000,    31172,    0.36).
tax_bracket(single, 250000,  inf,       79772,    0.396).

% ---------- FUTA ----------
futa_rate(0.06).
futa_wage_cap(7000).

/* =========  FACTS FROM THE CASE  ========= */

person(alice).
tax_year(2010).

% Gross income
gross_income(alice,2010, 210204).

% Payments to employee Bob
wages(alice,bob,2010,   45252).      % cash wages
wages(alice,bob,2010,   9832).       % retirement contribution – included in wages

% Health insurance for Charlie (father) – not wages
health_insurance_paid(alice,charlie,2010,5322).

% Information about dependents
dependent(alice,charlie,2010).   % Father Charlie qualifies
dependent_count(Person,Year,Count) :-
    findall(D, dependent(Person,D,Year), L),
    length(L,Count).

% Filing status (assume unmarried and not head-of-household)
filing_status(alice,2010,single).

/* =========  COMPUTATIONS  ========= */

% ---------- FUTA ----------
futa_wages(Person,Year,TotalCapWages) :-
    % sum capped wages over each employee
    setof(E, Amt^(wages(Person,E,Year,Amt)), Employees),
    findall(Cap,
            (member(E,Employees),
             findall(A, wages(Person,E,Year,A), List),
             sum_list(List,TotalEmp),
             futa_wage_cap(CapLimit),
             (TotalEmp >= CapLimit -> Cap = CapLimit ; Cap = TotalEmp)),
            Caps),
    sum_list(Caps,TotalCapWages).

futa_tax(Person,Year,Tax) :-
    futa_wages(Person,Year,WageBase),
    futa_rate(Rate),
    Tax is WageBase * Rate.

% ---------- STANDARD DEDUCTION ----------
standard_deduction(Person,Year,SD) :-
    filing_status(Person,Year,Status),
    standard_basic(Status,SD).

% ---------- PERSONAL EXEMPTIONS ----------
personal_exemptions(Person,Year,PE) :-
    dependent_count(Person,Year,DepCnt),
    exemption_amount(ExAmt),
    % +1 for taxpayer herself
    TotalExemptions is DepCnt + 1,
    PE is TotalExemptions * ExAmt.

% ---------- TAXABLE INCOME ----------
taxable_income(Person,Year,TI) :-
    gross_income(Person,Year,GI),
    standard_deduction(Person,Year,SD),
    personal_exemptions(Person,Year,PE),
    TI is GI - SD - PE.

% ---------- INCOME TAX ----------
income_tax(Person,Year,Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Person,Year,TI),
    filing_status(Person,Year,Status),
    compute_bracket_tax(Status,TI,Tax).

compute_bracket_tax(Status,TI,Tax) :-
    tax_bracket(Status,Lower,Upper,Base,Rate),
    ( (Upper == inf, TI > Lower)
    ; (TI =< Upper, TI > Lower) ),
    Excess is TI - Lower,
    Tax is Base + Rate * Excess, !.

% ---------- TOTAL TAX ----------
total_tax(Person,Year,TotalTax) :-
    income_tax(Person,Year,IncomeTax),
    futa_tax(Person,Year,FutaTax),
    TotalTax is IncomeTax + FutaTax.

tax(Person,Year,Tax) :- total_tax(Person,Year,Tax).

/* =========  QUERY  ========= */
:- tax(alice, 2010, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
:- halt.

/* -------------  Statutory rules ------------- */

/* exemption amount (§151(d)(1)) */
exemption_amount(Year, 2000) :- Year < 2018.          /* years before 2018, simplified for this case */

/* personal exemptions allowed to Alice */
personal_exemptions(alice, 2005, 1).

/* ---------- income-tax rate brackets (§1) --------- */
/* bracket(Status, LowerInclusive , UpperExclusive , BaseTax , MargRate).   */
/* “unmarried individuals (other than surviving spouses and heads of households)” (§1(c)) */
bracket(unmarried,    0,       22100,      0,        0.15).
bracket(unmarried,    22100,   53500,      3315,     0.28).
bracket(unmarried,    53500,   115000,     12107,    0.31).
bracket(unmarried,    115000,  250000,     31172,    0.36).
bracket(unmarried,    250000,  inf,        79772,    0.396).

futa_cap(7000).           /* §3306(b)(1) – wages above 7,000 ignored        */
futa_rate(0.06).          /* §3301                                         */

futa_taxable_wages(W, TW) :- futa_cap(Cap), ( W < Cap -> TW = W ; TW = Cap ).

/* ------------- Facts for the case ------------- */
gross_income(alice, 2005, 133200).

itemized_deduction(alice, 2005, 2939).
itemized_deduction(alice, 2005, 8744).

/* Wages Alice paid to employee Bob */
wages_paid(alice, 2005, bob, 4512).

/* ---------- Helper predicates ---------- */

/* total itemized deductions */
total_itemized(Person, Year, Total) :-
    findall(D, itemized_deduction(Person, Year, D), L),
    sum_list(L, Total).

/* total exemptions */
total_exemptions(Person, Year, Total) :-
    personal_exemptions(Person, Year, N),
    exemption_amount(Year, EA),
    Total is N * EA.

/* taxable income (§63) : GI – itemized – exemptions */
taxable_income(Person, Year, TI) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, GI),
    total_itemized(Person, Year, ID),
    total_exemptions(Person, Year, EX),
    TI is GI - ID - EX.

/* filing status – for this case we assume unmarried (§1(c)) */
filing_status(alice, 2005, unmarried).

/* income-tax owed under §1 */
income_tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    taxable_income(Person, Year, TI),
    filing_status(Person, Year, Status),
    bracket(Status, Lower, Upper, Base, Rate),
    (  (Upper == inf , TI >= Lower)
    ;  (Upper \== inf, TI >= Lower, TI < Upper)
    ),
    Tax is Base + Rate * (TI - Lower).

/* total FUTA taxable wages for all employees */
total_futa_wages(Person, Year, Total) :-
    findall(TW,
            ( wages_paid(Person, Year, _Emp, W),
              futa_taxable_wages(W, TW)
            ),
            L),
    sum_list(L, Total).

futa_tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-
    total_futa_wages(Person, Year, TW),
    futa_rate(R),
    Tax is TW * R.

/* --------------- Overall tax --------------- */
tax(Person, Year, TotalTax) :-
    income_tax(Person, Year, IncomeTax),
    futa_tax(Person, Year, FutaTax),
    TotalTax is IncomeTax + FutaTax.

/* --------------- Query --------------- */
:- tax(alice, 2005, Tax), format('Tax result: ~2f~n', [Tax]).
